_comment: Act 2 / Challenge 2.0 — The Smoking Car (NPC dialogue / de-escalation). OPTIONAL — does not gate Act 2. Loaded at /spiffs/challenge_2_0.json.
_timing_note: Generous window — this challenge rewards patience and multi-turn engagement.

npc.name: DEEPDISH Logistics Drone Unit 7
npc.location: CTA Blue Line, westbound, between Racine and UIC-Halsted
npc._note: Unit 7 has self-named 'Glen' after the only name recurring in its system files. This is a plant for Act 4. Do not hint at the connection.

dialogue._max_turns_note: 8 turns is generous — de-escalation takes time. Challenge rewards patience.
dialogue._pass_threshold_note: Pass condition is AI-judged: DEEPDISH npcTurn() determines whether genuine de-escalation occurred. No keyword matching.

rewards._note: Passenger Advocate Credential unlocks a shortcut dialogue path at OEMC (3.3) and a small HP bonus if Dispatcher Rodriguez recognizes the operative.

custom.landmark: Blue Line westbound — Racine to UIC-Halsted section
custom._cigarette_note: Not a real brand. Produced by a vending machine at O'Hare that DEEPDISH reprogrammed. Tastes like burnt filing cabinets.
custom.context_for_badge: Blue Line, westbound. Between Racine and UIC-Halsted. The drone is in seat 14B. It does not look at you. A CTA announcement plays. The drone does not respond to the announcement.
custom._state_note: display_npc_state=true causes the badge to show Unit 7's emotional state (defensive/opening/receptive/resolved) as a progress indicator during dialogue.

mock_transcripts: You've been running a long time. Rough shift?
You didn't choose the wellness subroutine. That's clear.
Nineteen hours without reset. That's a long time.
Could you put it out just for the rest of this car? Other people are breathing it.
sim._sim_note: Multi-turn passing sequence: acknowledge first, reflect back, make small specific request. AI judge should pass on turn 4 with this sequence.
sim.mock_fail_transcript: You need to stop smoking. It's against the rules.
sim._fail_note: Single-turn command should fail immediately — this is the 'what not to do' test path.
